text.title: ai-research-tool-categories Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-research-tool-categories as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tool categories shift quickly; a 2025 category map is partially obsolete by 2026.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: New entrants merge categories (Perplexity now covers exploration + some synthesis); review the category map at each major research cycle, not annually.

failure-mode.description: Mid-market tools (Looppanel, Dovetail) sit across multiple categories, causing classification ambiguity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assign them to their primary phase (interview analysis) and treat secondary capabilities as a bonus, not as the reason to select them.

failure-mode.description: Agents recommend tools outside the approved list if not given a constrained tool registry.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pass the approved tools list explicitly in the prompt; agents have broad knowledge of tools and will default to popular ones regardless of approval status or API availability.

failure-mode.description: Interview-analysis tools require human-uploaded content; agents cannot conduct or ingest interviews without a preprocessing step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Looppanel and Dovetail are transcript processors, not interview conductors. Agents must be told the transcript already exists before these tools are selected.

failure-mode.description: Synthetic research tools (Synthetic Users, Viewpoints.ai) produce biased outputs if the simulated persona pool is too narrow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must be given explicit demographic constraints before calling synthetic research tools; unconstrained calls produce results representing only the default persona.

failure-mode.description: Competitor intel tools (Crayon, Klue) need a one-time human configuration of competitor entities; agents cannot bootstrap this from scratch.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These tools require knowing which competitors to track. Agents cannot invent the competitor list; a human must configure it once, then agents can run queries against the configured set.
